title: Port Changes Mutate — validateChangeAll
description: Client port method for DAG batch validation (`POST .../validate-all`).
context: Client port method for DAG batch validation (`POST .../validate-all`).

rules:
`SpecdDataPort` MUST declare:

```typescript
validateChangeAll(
  name: string,
  input?: { artifactId?: string },
  signal?: AbortSignal,
): Promise<ValidateBatchResultDto>
```
`adapter-memory-specd-data` MAY return `{ passed: true, total: 0, results: [] }` for UI tests without a kernel.
`validateChange(name, { specId?, artifactId? })` MUST continue to call `POST .../validate` only.

scenarios.name: validateChange stays single-step
when: validateChange is called with { specId, artifactId }

scenarios.name: remote adapter path — primary path
when: adapter-remote-specd-data MUST POST /changes/{name}/validate-all with JSON body input
then: AND no forbidden side effects occur

scenarios.name: remote adapter path — guard path
given: inputs that stress the requirement boundary
then: errors or skips are explicit and documented

scenarios.name: memory adapter stub — primary path
when: adapter-memory-specd-data MAY return { passed: true, total: 0,
then: AND no forbidden side effects occur

scenarios.name: memory adapter stub — guard path
given: inputs that stress the requirement boundary
then: errors or skips are explicit and documented

scenarios.name: validateChange remains single-step — primary path
when: validateChange(name, { specId?, artifactId? }) MUST continue to
then: AND no forbidden side effects occur

scenarios.name: validateChange remains single-step — guard path
given: inputs that stress the requirement boundary
then: errors or skips are explicit and documented
